KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
text.Rotherham: Paul and Silvanus and Timothy—unto the assembly of Thessalonians, in God our Father and Lord Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
text.NABRE: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
text.DRB: Paul and Sylvanus and Timothee, to the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Favour unto you, and peace, from God [our] Father and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: Grace unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: grace to you and peace from God [our] Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: We must always give thanks to God for you, brothers and sisters, as is right, because your faith is growing abundantly, and the love of everyone of you for one another is increasing.
text.Rotherham: We are bound, to be giving thanks, unto God, continually, concerning you, brethren, even as it is, meet; because your faith groweth exceedingly, and the love of each one of you all one to another aboundeth,
text.KJV: We are bound to thank God always for you, brethren, as it is meet, because that your faith groweth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you all toward each other aboundeth;
text.NABRE: Thanksgiving. We ought to thank God always for you, brothers, as is fitting, because your faith flourishes ever more, and the love of every one of you for one another grows ever greater.
text.DRB: We ought to give thanks always to God for you, brethren, so as meet is, because your faith increaseth exceedingly, and the charity of every one of you aboundeth towards each other:

text.NRSV: Therefore we ourselves boast of you among the churches of God for your steadfastness and faith during all your persecutions and the afflictions that you are enduring.
text.Rotherham: So that, we ourselves, in you, are boasting, in the assemblies of God, over your endurance and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations which ye are sustaining:—
text.KJV: So that we ourselves glory in you in the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations that ye endure:
text.NABRE: Accordingly, we ourselves boast of you in the churches of God regarding your endurance and faith in all your persecutions and the afflictions you endure.
text.DRB: so that we ourselves also glory in you in the churches of God, for your patience, and faith in all your persecutions and tribulations, which you sustain

text.NRSV: This is evidence of the righteous judgment of God, and is intended to make you worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you are also suffering.
text.Rotherham: A proof of the righteous judgment of God, to the end ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, in behalf of which ye are also suffering:—
text.KJV: Which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which ye also suffer:
text.NABRE: This is evidence of the just judgment of God, so that you may be considered worthy of the kingdom of God for which you are suffering.
text.DRB: for an example of the just judgement of God, that you may be counted worthy of the Kingdom of God, for the which also you suffer.

text.NRSV: For it is indeed just of God to repay with affliction those who afflict you,
text.Rotherham: If, at least, it is a, righteous thing with God, to recompense, affliction, unto them that afflict you,
text.KJV: Seeing it is a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you;
text.NABRE: For it is surely just on God’s part to repay with afflictions those who are afflicting you,
text.DRB: If yet it be just with God to repay tribulation, to them that vex you:

text.NRSV: and to give relief to the afflicted as well as to us, when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with his mighty angels
text.Rotherham: And, unto you that are afflicted, release, with us,—by the revealing of the Lord Jesus from heaven, with his messengers of power,
text.KJV: And to you who are troubled rest with us, when the Lord Jesus shall be revealed from heaven with his mighty angels,
text.NABRE: and to grant rest along with us to you who are undergoing afflictions, at the revelation of the Lord Jesus from heaven with his mighty angels,
text.DRB: and to you that are vexed, rest with us in the revelation of our Lord Jesus from Heaven with the Angels of his power,

text.NRSV: in flaming fire, inflicting vengeance on those who do not know God and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: In a fiery flame; holding forth vengeance—against them that refuse to know God, and them who decline to hearken unto the glad-message of our Lord Jesus,
text.KJV: In flaming fire taking vengeance on them that know not God, and that obey not the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ:
text.NABRE: in blazing fire, inflicting punishment on those who do not acknowledge God and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus.
text.DRB: in flame of fire, giving revenge to them that know not God, and that obey not the Gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: These will suffer the punishment of eternal destruction, separated from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his might,
text.Rotherham: Who, indeed, a penalty, shall pay—age-abiding destruction from the face of the Lord and from the glory of his might—
text.KJV: Who shall be punished with everlasting destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power;
text.NABRE: These will pay the penalty of eternal ruin, separated from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of his power,
text.DRB: Who shall suffer eternal pains in destruction, from the face of our Lord and from the glory of his power:

text.NRSV: when he comes to be glorified by his saints and to be marveled at on that day among all who have believed, because our testimony to you was believed.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever he shall come, to be made all-glorious in his saints, and to be marvelled at in all who believed,—because our witness unto you was believed,—in that day.
text.KJV: When he shall come to be glorified in his saints, and to be admired in all them that believe (because our testimony among you was believed) in that day.
text.NABRE: when he comes to be glorified among his holy ones and to be marveled at on that day among all who have believed, for our testimony to you was believed.
text.DRB: when he shall come to be glorified in his saints, and to be made marvelous in all them that have believed, because our testimony concerning you was credited in that day.

text.NRSV: To this end we always pray for you, asking that our God will make you worthy of his call and will fulfill by his power every good resolve and work of faith,
text.Rotherham: Unto which end, we are also praying continually for you, that our God may count, you, worthy of your calling, and fulfil every good-pleasure of goodness and work of faith, with power,—
text.KJV: Wherefore also we pray always for you, that our God would count you worthy of this calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of his goodness, and the work of faith with power:
text.NABRE: Prayer. To this end, we always pray for you, that our God may make you worthy of his calling and powerfully bring to fulfillment every good purpose and every effort of faith,
text.DRB: Wherein also we pray always for you, that our God make you worthy of his vocation, and accomplish all the good pleasure of his goodness & the work of faith in power,

text.NRSV: so that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: That the name of our Lord Jesus may be made all-glorious in you, and, ye, in him, According to the favour of our God and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you, and you in him, in accord with the grace of our God and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: that the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and you in him, according to the grace of our God, and of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: As to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our being gathered together to him, we beg you, brothers and sisters,
text.Rotherham: But we request you, brethren,—in behalf of the Presence of our Lord Jesus Christ, and our gathering together unto him,
text.KJV: Now we beseech you, brethren, by the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and by our gathering together unto him,
text.NABRE: We ask you, brothers, with regard to the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our assembling with him,
text.DRB: And we desire you, brethren, by the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, & of our congregation into him;

text.NRSV: not to be quickly shaken in mind or alarmed, either by spirit or by word or by letter, as though from us, to the effect that the day of the Lord is already here.
text.Rotherham: That ye be not quickly tossed from your mind, nor be put in alarm—either by spirit, or by discourse, or by letter as by us, as that the day of the Lord, hath set in:
text.KJV: That ye be not soon shaken in mind, or be troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by letter as from us, as that the day of Christ is at hand.
text.NABRE: not to be shaken out of your minds suddenly, or to be alarmed either by a “spirit,” or by an oral statement, or by a letter allegedly from us to the effect that the day of the Lord is at hand.
text.DRB: that you be not easily moved from your sense, nor be terrified, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by epistle as sent by us, as though the day of our Lord were at hand.

text.NRSV: Let no one deceive you in any way; for that day will not come unless the rebellion comes first and the lawless one is revealed, the one destined for destruction.
text.Rotherham: That no one may cheat, you, in any one respect. Because [that day will not set in] —except the revolt come first, and there he revealed the man of lawlessness, the son of destruction,
text.KJV: Let no man deceive you by any means: for that day shall not come, except there come a falling away first, and that man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition;
text.NABRE: Let no one deceive you in any way. For unless the apostasy comes first and the lawless one is revealed, the one doomed to perdition,
text.DRB: Let no man seduce you by any means, for unless there come a revolt first, and the man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition,

text.NRSV: He opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god or object of worship, so that he takes his seat in the temple of God, declaring himself to be God.
text.Rotherham: The one who opposeth and exalteth himself on high against every one called God, or an object of worship; so that he, within the sanctuary of God, shall take his seat, showeth himself forth, that he is, God:—
text.KJV: Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.
text.NABRE: who opposes and exalts himself above every so-called god and object of worship, so as to seat himself in the temple of God, claiming that he is a god—
text.DRB: which is an adversary & is extolled above all that is called God, or that is worshipped, so that he sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself as though he were God.

text.NRSV: Do you not remember that I told you these things when I was still with you?
text.Rotherham: Remember ye not, that, while I was yet with you, these very things, I was telling you?
text.KJV: Remember ye not, that, when I was yet with you, I told you these things?
text.NABRE: do you not recall that while I was still with you I told you these things?
text.DRB: Remember you not, that when I was yet with you, I told you these things?

text.NRSV: And you know what is now restraining him, so that he may be revealed when his time comes.
text.Rotherham: And, what now restraineth, ye know, to the end he may be revealed in his own fitting time;
text.KJV: And now ye know what withholdeth that he might be revealed in his time.
text.NABRE: And now you know what is restraining, that he may be revealed in his time.
text.DRB: And now what letteth, you know: that he may be revealed in his time.

text.NRSV: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work, but only until the one who now restrains it is removed.
text.Rotherham: For, the secret, of lawlessness, already, is inwardly working itself,—only, until, he that restraineth at present, shall be gone, out of the midst:
text.KJV: For the mystery of iniquity doth already work: only he who now letteth will let, until he be taken out of the way.
text.NABRE: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work. But the one who restrains is to do so only for the present, until he is removed from the scene.
text.DRB: (For now the mystery of iniquity worketh: only that he which now holdeth, do hold, until he be taken out of the way.)

text.NRSV: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will destroy with the breath of his mouth, annihilating him by the manifestation of his coming.
text.Rotherham: And, then, shall be revealed the lawless one,—whom, the Lord [Jesus], will slay with the Spirit of his mouth, and paralyse with the forthshining of his Presence:—
text.KJV: And then shall that Wicked be revealed, whom the Lord shall consume with the spirit of his mouth, and shall destroy with the brightness of his coming:
text.NABRE: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord [Jesus] will kill with the breath of his mouth and render powerless by the manifestation of his coming,
text.DRB: And then that wicked one shall be revealed whom our Lord Jesus shall kill with the spirit of his mouth; and shall destroy with the manifestation of his advent, him,

text.NRSV: The coming of the lawless one is apparent in the working of Satan, who uses all power, signs, lying wonders,
text.Rotherham: Whose, presence, [shall be] according to an inworking of Satan, with all manner of mighty work and signs and wonders of falsehood,
text.KJV: Even him, whose coming is after the working of Satan with all power and signs and lying wonders,
text.NABRE: the one whose coming springs from the power of Satan in every mighty deed and in signs and wonders that lie,
text.DRB: whose coming is according to the operation of Satan, in all power, and lying signs and wonders;

text.NRSV: and every kind of wicked deception for those who are perishing, because they refused to love the truth and so be saved.
text.Rotherham: And with all manner of deceit of unrighteousness, in them who are destroying themselves, because, the love of the truth, they did not welcome, that they might be saved;—
text.KJV: And with all deceivableness of unrighteousness in them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth, that they might be saved.
text.NABRE: and in every wicked deceit for those who are perishing because they have not accepted the love of truth so that they may be saved.
text.DRB: and in all seducing of iniquity to them that perish; for that they have not received the charity of the truth that they might be saved.

text.NRSV: For this reason God sends them a powerful delusion, leading them to believe what is false,
text.Rotherham: And, for this cause, God sendeth them an inworking of error, to the end they should believe in the falsehood,—
text.KJV: And for this cause God shall send them strong delusion, that they should believe a lie:
text.NABRE: Therefore, God is sending them a deceiving power so that they may believe the lie,
text.DRB: Therefore God will send them the operation of error, to believe lying:

text.NRSV: so that all who have not believed the truth but took pleasure in unrighteousness will be condemned.
text.Rotherham: In order that should be judged who would not believe in the truth, but were well-pleased with the unrighteousness.
text.KJV: That they all might be damned who believed not the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
text.NABRE: that all who have not believed the truth but have approved wrongdoing may be condemned.
text.DRB: that all may be judged which have not believed the truth, but have consented to iniquity.

text.NRSV: But we must always give thanks to God for you, brothers and sisters beloved by the Lord, because God chose you as the first fruits for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and through belief in the truth.
text.Rotherham: But, we, are bound to give thanks unto God continually concerning you, brethren beloved by the Lord, for that God chose you, from the beginning, unto salvation, in sanctification of spirit and belief of truth,—
text.KJV: But we are bound to give thanks alway to God for you, brethren beloved of the Lord, because God hath from the beginning chosen you to salvation through sanctification of the Spirit and belief of the truth:
text.NABRE: But we ought to give thanks to God for you always, brothers loved by the Lord, because God chose you as the firstfruits for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in truth.
text.DRB: But we ought to give thanks to God always for you, brethren beloved of God, that he hath chosen you first-fruits unto salvation, in sanctification of spirit and faith of the truth:

text.NRSV: For this purpose he called you through our proclamation of the good news, so that you may obtain the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Unto which he called you, through means of our glad-message, unto an acquiring of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: Whereunto he called you by our gospel, to the obtaining of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: To this end he has [also] called you through our gospel to possess the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: into the which also he hath called you by our Gospel, unto the purchasing of the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm and hold fast to the traditions that you were taught by us, either by word of mouth or by our letter.
text.Rotherham: Hence, then, brethren, stand firm, and hold fast the instructions which ye were taught—whether through discourse, or through our letter.
text.KJV: Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle.
text.NABRE: Therefore, brothers, stand firm and hold fast to the traditions that you were taught, either by an oral statement or by a letter of ours.
text.DRB: Therefore, brethren, stand; and hold the traditions which you have learned, whether it be by word, or by our epistle.

text.NRSV: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who loved us and through grace gave us eternal comfort and good hope,
text.Rotherham: But may, our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and our God and Father,—Who hath loved you, and given you age-abiding consolation and good hope by favour,
text.KJV: Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God, even our Father, which hath loved us, and hath given us everlasting consolation and good hope through grace,
text.NABRE: May our Lord Jesus Christ himself and God our Father, who has loved us and given us everlasting encouragement and good hope through his grace,
text.DRB: And our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God and our Father which hath loved us, and hath given eternal consolation, and good hope in grace,

text.NRSV: comfort your hearts and strengthen them in every good work and word.
text.Rotherham: Console your hearts, and confirm you in every good work and word!
text.KJV: Comfort your hearts, and stablish you in every good word and work.
text.NABRE: encourage your hearts and strengthen them in every good deed and word.
text.DRB: exhort your hearts and confirm you in every good work and word.

text.NRSV: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us, so that the word of the Lord may spread rapidly and be glorified everywhere, just as it is among you,
text.Rotherham: For the rest, brethren, be praying for us,—that, the word of the Lord, may be running, and gaining glory, according as [it did] even with you;
text.KJV: Finally, brethren, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may have free course, and be glorified, even as it is with you:
text.NABRE: Finally, brothers, pray for us, so that the word of the Lord may speed forward and be glorified, as it did among you,
text.DRB: For the rest, brethren, pray for us, that the word of God may have course and be glorified, as also with you:

text.NRSV: and that we may be rescued from wicked and evil people; for not all have faith.
text.Rotherham: And that we may be rescued from the presuming and wicked men; for, not all, hold the faith.
text.KJV: And that we may be delivered from unreasonable and wicked men: for all men have not faith.
text.NABRE: and that we may be delivered from perverse and wicked people, for not all have faith.
text.DRB: and that we may be delivered from importunate and naughty men. For all men have not faith.

text.NRSV: But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, is the Lord,—who will confirm you, and guard you from the wicked one:
text.KJV: But the Lord is faithful, who shall stablish you, and keep you from evil.
text.NABRE: But the Lord is faithful; he will strengthen you and guard you from the evil one.
text.DRB: But our Lord is faithful, who will confirm and keep you from evil.

text.NRSV: And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you are doing and will go on doing the things that we command.
text.Rotherham: We are persuaded, however, in the Lord, as touching you,—that, what things we give in charge, Ye [both] are doing and will do;—
text.KJV: And we have confidence in the Lord touching you, that ye both do and will do the things which we command you.
text.NABRE: We are confident of you in the Lord that what we instruct you, you [both] are doing and will continue to do.
text.DRB: And we have confidence of you in our Lord, that the things which we command, both you do, and will do.

text.NRSV: May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the steadfastness of Christ.
text.Rotherham: But may, the Lord, guide your hearts into the love of God, and into the endurance of the Christ.
text.KJV: And the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God, and into the patient waiting for Christ.
text.NABRE: May the Lord direct your hearts to the love of God and to the endurance of Christ.
text.DRB: And our Lord direct your hearts in the charity of God, and patience of Christ.

text.NRSV: Now we command you, beloved, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, to keep away from believers who are living in idleness and not according to the tradition that they received from us.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, we charge you, brethren,—that, in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, ye be withdrawing yourselves from every brother—who, in a disorderly way, doth walk, and not according to the instruction which ye received from us.
text.KJV: Now we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye withdraw yourselves from every brother that walketh disorderly, and not after the tradition which he received of us.
text.NABRE: Neglect of Work. We instruct you, brothers, in the name of [our] Lord Jesus Christ, to shun any brother who conducts himself in a disorderly way and not according to the tradition they received from us.
text.DRB: And we denounce unto you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw yourselves from every brother walking inordinately, and not according to the tradition which they have received of us.

text.NRSV: For you yourselves know how you ought to imitate us; we were not idle when we were with you,
text.Rotherham: For, ye yourselves, know, how needful it is to be imitating us, in that we were not disorderly among you,
text.KJV: For yourselves know how ye ought to follow us: for we behaved not ourselves disorderly among you;
text.NABRE: For you know how one must imitate us. For we did not act in a disorderly way among you,
text.DRB: For yourselves know how you ought to imitate us: for we have not been unquiet among you:

text.NRSV: and we did not eat anyone’s bread without paying for it; but with toil and labor we worked night and day, so that we might not burden any of you.
text.Rotherham: Nor, as a free-gift, did eat, bread, from anyone, but, with toil and hardship, night and day, working,—that we might not burden any of you;—
text.KJV: Neither did we eat any man’s bread for nought; but wrought with labour and travail night and day, that we might not be chargeable to any of you:
text.NABRE: nor did we eat food received free from anyone. On the contrary, in toil and drudgery, night and day we worked, so as not to burden any of you.
text.DRB: neither have we eaten bread of any man gratis, but in labour & in toil night and day working, lest we should burden any of you.

text.NRSV: This was not because we do not have that right, but in order to give you an example to imitate.
text.Rotherham: Not because we have not authority, but that, ourselves, as an ensample, we might hold forth unto you,—to the end ye might he imitating us.
text.KJV: Not because we have not power, but to make ourselves an ensample unto you to follow us.
text.NABRE: Not that we do not have the right. Rather, we wanted to present ourselves as a model for you, so that you might imitate us.
text.DRB: Not as though we had not authority: but that we might give ourselves a pattern unto you for to imitate us.

text.NRSV: For even when we were with you, we gave you this command: Anyone unwilling to work should not eat.
text.Rotherham: For, even when we were with you, this, were we giving in charge unto you,—that, if any will not work, neither let him eat!
text.KJV: For even when we were with you, this we commanded you, that if any would not work, neither should he eat.
text.NABRE: In fact, when we were with you, we instructed you that if anyone was unwilling to work, neither should that one eat.
text.DRB: For also when we were with you, this we denounced to you, that if any will not work, neither let him eat.

text.NRSV: For we hear that some of you are living in idleness, mere busybodies, not doing any work.
text.Rotherham: We hear, in fact, of some who are walking among you in a disorderly way, at nothing, working, yet too busily working!
text.KJV: For we hear that there are some which walk among you disorderly, working not at all, but are busybodies.
text.NABRE: We hear that some are conducting themselves among you in a disorderly way, by not keeping busy but minding the business of others.
text.DRB: For we have heard of certain among you that walk unquietly, working nothing, but curiously meddling.

text.NRSV: Now such persons we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ to do their work quietly and to earn their own living.
text.Rotherham: Now, such as these, we charge and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ,—that, with quietness, working, their own bread, they be eating.
text.KJV: Now them that are such we command and exhort by our Lord Jesus Christ, that with quietness they work, and eat their own bread.
text.NABRE: Such people we instruct and urge in the Lord Jesus Christ to work quietly and to eat their own food.
text.DRB: And to them that be such we denounce, & beseech them in our Lord Jesus Christ, that working with silence, they eat their own bread.

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, do not be weary in doing what is right.
text.Rotherham: But, ye, brethren, be not weary in well-doing;—
text.KJV: But ye, brethren, be not weary in well doing.
text.NABRE: But you, brothers, do not be remiss in doing good.
text.DRB: But you, brethren faint not well-doing.

text.NRSV: Take note of those who do not obey what we say in this letter; have nothing to do with them, so that they may be ashamed.
text.Rotherham: And, if anyone be not giving ear unto our word through means of this letter, on this one, set a mark—not to be mixing yourselves up with him,—that he may be reproved;
text.KJV: And if any man obey not our word by this epistle, note that man, and have no company with him, that he may be ashamed.
text.NABRE: If anyone does not obey our word as expressed in this letter, take note of this person not to associate with him, that he may be put to shame.
text.DRB: And if any obey not our word, note him by an epistle:

text.NRSV: Do not regard them as enemies, but warn them as believers.
text.Rotherham: And, not as an enemy, be esteeming him, but be admonishing him, as a brother.
text.KJV: Yet count him not as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
text.NABRE: Do not regard him as an enemy but admonish him as a brother.
text.DRB: and do not company with him, that he may be confounded: and do not esteem him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.

text.NRSV: Now may the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with all of you.
text.Rotherham: But may, the Lord of peace himself, give you peace, always, in every way. The Lord, be with you all.
text.KJV: Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord be with you all.
text.NABRE: May the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with all of you.
text.DRB: And the Lord of peace himself give you everlasting peace in every place. Our Lord be with you all.

text.NRSV: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. This is the mark in every letter of mine; it is the way I write.
text.Rotherham: The salutation of Paul—with my own hand, which is a sign in every letter: thus, I write.
text.KJV: The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write.
text.NABRE: This is the sign in every letter; this is how I write.
text.DRB: The salutation, with mine own hand, Paul's: which is a sign in every epistle. So I write.

text.NRSV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
text.Rotherham: The favour of our Lord Jesus Christ, be with you all.
text.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen. The second epistle to the Thessalonians was written from Athens.
text.NABRE: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with all of you.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
